description: Fermi estimate of the heise-comment causal chain 'world too complex -> loss of trust -> bad collective decisions', global scope ~2005-2025, decomposed into four multiplicative links so each assumption is independently criticisable. Each competing cause of trust decline now carries a causal-direction qualifier triple (cause to effect / bidirectional) and an evidence grade, backed by named longitudinal/experimental studies in sources. The sign-determining link s_komplex is downweighted because complexity is the only channel without causal-identified longitudinal support. Provenance per value via measured value / assumed value triples; numbers reproduced by calc-validation.

words.name: decline
words.description: downward change of a quantity over a period

words.name: share
words.description: attributable proportion of an effect

words.name: attribution
words.description: assignment of an effect to a cause

words.name: effect
words.description: the outcome of a cause

words.name: incompetence
words.description: institutional under-performance and broken promises

words.name: benevolence
words.description: government acting in citizens' interests

words.name: avoidable
words.description: causally preventable, not merely correlated

words.name: baseline
words.description: reference magnitude against which a loss is measured

words.name: waking
words.description: waking, net-positive part of lived time

words.name: direction
words.description: causal direction of an association between two factors

words.name: grade
words.description: evidence grade of a causal claim

words.name: HTP
words.description: Happy Time Point, the zukunft.com DALY-extended wellbeing unit

words.name: measured
words.description: qualifier: value taken from a measurement/observation

words.name: assumed
words.description: qualifier: value is an explicit assumption, not a measurement

words.name: value
words.description: a stored number; reference the provenance qualifier triple, not this bare word

words.name: low
words.description: lower bound of an assumed band

words.name: high
words.description: upper bound of an assumed band

words.name: centre
words.description: geometric centre of a band

words.name: strong
words.description: contra-thesis variant: complexity treated as a primary driver

words.name: evidence
words.description: evidence-adjusted variant after the literature review

words.name: A
words.description: evidence grade A: longitudinal/experimental with causal identification

words.name: B
words.description: evidence grade B: mixed evidence

words.name: C
words.description: evidence grade C: predominantly correlational

words.name: global
words.description: global scope

triples.name: measured value
triples.description: provenance qualifier: number comes from a measurement

triples.name: assumed value
triples.description: provenance qualifier: number is an explicit assumption

triples.name: cause to effect
triples.description: directed causal channel: the factor causes trust decline

triples.name: bidirectional
triples.description: reciprocal channel: factor and trust each cause the other

triples.name: waking welfare fraction
triples.description: fraction of lived time that is waking and net-positive

triples.name: bad steering fraction
triples.description: share of global welfare lost to collectively bad steering

triples.name: bad steering loss
triples.description: R_total: welfare lost per year to bad collective decisions

triples.name: complexity share
triples.description: s_komplex: share of the trust decline attributable to complexity

triples.name: complexity trust attribution
triples.description: the attribution of trust decline specifically to complexity

triples.name: inequality via efficacy
triples.description: identified mechanism: inequality lowers efficacy which lowers trust

triples.name: distrust mediation share
triples.description: t_misstrauen: fraction of the complexity effect routed via distrust

triples.name: chain HTP loss
triples.description: result: HTP/yr loss attributable to the full chain

sources.name: Bienstman 2024
sources.description: Bienstman, Rummens & Marx (2024). Explaining the democratic malaise: inequality, external efficacy and political trust. European Journal of Political Research. FE + causal mediation: inequality lowers trust via efficacy.

sources.name: Devine 2025
sources.description: Devine (2025). The causes of perceived government trustworthiness. European Journal of Political Research. Experimental: benevolence is the strongest trust determinant; competence and integrity secondary.

sources.name: Van de Walle Bouckaert 2003
sources.description: Van de Walle & Bouckaert (2003). Public Service Performance and Trust in Government: The Problem of Causality. Int. J. Public Administration 26(8-9). Performance and trust are reciprocal.

sources.name: Tsfati misinformation review 2023
sources.description: Ognyanova et al. / Harvard Misinformation Review (2023). Fake-news exposure linked to lower media trust in behavioural-tracking data; single-exposure experiments often null; reverse causality possible.

sources.name: Van Remoortere Vliegenthart 2025
sources.description: Van Remoortere & Vliegenthart (2025). Affective polarization and political (dis)trust. Cross-lagged panel: higher trust lowers polarisation; polarisation does not significantly raise distrust.

sources.name: Nieuwenhuis inequality trust 2017
sources.description: Nieuwenhuis (2017). Less equal, less trusting? Longitudinal effects of income inequality on trust in US states 1973-2012. Social Science Research. Longitudinal effect weak and sensitive to time controls.

sources.name: UN World Population Prospects 2024
sources.description: UN DESA, World Population Prospects 2024, world total ~8.0e9.

sources.name: zukunft.com Fermi assumption
sources.description: explicit modelling assumption set by the analyst for this Fermi estimate, not a measurement

formulas.name: bad steering loss formula
formulas.expression: "bad steering loss" = "global welfare baseline" * "bad steering fraction"

formulas.name: chain HTP loss formula
formulas.expression: "chain HTP loss" = "bad steering loss" * "complexity share" * "distrust mediation share" * "avoidable share"

calc-validation.note: geometric centre with original s_komplex 0.0866 and q 0.3464
evidence-adjusted: s_komplex 0.0658 (no causal-identified longitudinal support) and q 0.2632 (reciprocal channels lower avoidability); factor 0.577 vs original centre
contra-thesis: complexity as primary driver, s_komplex lifted to 0.30; only 3.46x the original centre, still not dominant

components.name: Title theses
components.description: page title: the thesis being weighed against its anti-theses

components.name: table theses probability and anti theses probability
components.description: one row per competing cause of the trust decline: attributed share, causal direction, strength, evidence grade, source; the thesis channel (complexity) is the last row. Renders the 'Causal direction and evidence grade per channel' table of the markdown.

components.name: table chain links and result
components.description: one row per multiplicative chain link with low/centre/high columns; last row is the chain result. Renders the 'Chain links and result' table of the markdown.

components.name: table sensitivity of the sign determining assumption
components.description: one row per scenario variant of the sign-determining link s_komplex: base, evidence-adjusted, strong; columns s_komplex, avoidable share, chain result, factor vs base. Renders the 'Sensitivity' table of the markdown.

views.name: theses/antitheses
views.description: overview of the probability of a thesis with the anti theses

components.name: table theses probability and anti theses probability
table sensitivity of the sign determining assumption

view_validations.md:
# Fermi: complexity → trust → bad decisions
 
Companion overview to `fermi_complexity_trust.json`. Global scope, ~2005–2025.
All numbers reproduced by the `calc-validation` block in the JSON. Provenance is
carried per value via the `measured value` / `assumed value` qualifier triples;
each competing cause additionally carries a `cause to effect` / `bidirectional`
direction triple and an `evidence grade A/B/C` triple.
 
## Causal direction and evidence grade per channel
 
Grade A = longitudinal/experimental with causal identification, B = mixed,
C = predominantly correlational.
 
| channel | share (mid) | dominant direction | strength | grade | source |
| --- | ---: | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
| incompetence / broken promises | 0.32 | cause → trust, but reciprocal | strong | A–B | Devine 2025; Van de Walle Bouckaert 2003 |
| economic (inequality) | 0.27 | cause → trust, via efficacy | medium–strong | A | Bienstman 2024; Nieuwenhuis 2017 |
| media / misinformation | 0.20 | mixed, weaker than assumed | weak–medium | B | Tsfati misinformation review 2023 |
| polarisation / identity | 0.15 | bidirectional, partly trust → polarisation | medium | B | Van Remoortere Vliegenthart 2025 |
| complexity | 0.09 | weak, mostly an amplifier | very weak | C | zukunft.com Fermi assumption |
 
Shares sum to ~1.03; causes overlap, so they are not disjoint.
 
Key finding from the literature review: complexity is the only channel **without**
a causal-identified longitudinal study supporting a complexity → trust arrow,
while inequality → trust (via efficacy) and benevolence/competence → trust are
causally identified. Several channels (performance ↔ trust, polarisation ↔ trust)
are reciprocal, so part of what the comment reads as a directed
"complexity → distrust → bad decision" arrow is in fact a feedback loop.
 
## Chain links and result (HTP per year)
 
`chain = R_total * s_komplex * t_misstrauen * q_fehlentscheid`
 
| link | symbol | low | centre | high | role |
| --- | --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | --- |
| welfare lost to bad steering | R_total | 2.4e7 | 7.6e7 | 2.4e8 | scale |
| complexity attribution share | s_komplex | 0.05 | 0.087 | 0.15 | sign-determining |
| distrust mediation | t_misstrauen | 0.3 | 0.46 | 0.7 | mechanism |
| avoidable (causal) fraction | q_fehlentscheid | 0.2 | 0.35 | 0.6 | causality |
| chain result | HTP/yr | 7.2e4 | 1.0e6 | 1.5e7 | product |
 
## Sensitivity of the sign-determining assumption
 
| variant | s_komplex | q | result (centre) | factor vs base |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| base | 0.087 | 0.346 | 1.0e6 HTP/yr | 1.00x |
| evidence-adjusted | 0.066 | 0.263 | 6.0e5 HTP/yr | 0.58x |
| strong (complexity primary) | 0.30 | 0.346 | 3.6e6 HTP/yr | 3.46x |
 
The evidence-adjusted centre lowers the original by factor 0.58, reflecting the
absence of causal-identified support for complexity and the reciprocal nature of
the distrust channel. Even the strong variant lifts the result only 3.46x without
making the thesis dominant: complexity is a third-order amplifier (5–15 %), not a
primary driver of the trust decline.
